api/Lunaris.Config.IConfig.html.title: Interface IConfig | Lunaris
api/Lunaris.Config.IConfig.html.summary: Interface IConfig Namespace Lunaris.Config Assembly Lunaris.dll public interface IConfig Properties OnAnyChanged Subscribe to ALL changes. Action<string, object> OnAnyChanged { get; set; } Property Value Action<string, object> Examples Config.OnAnyChanged += (key, val) => { }; Remarks Warning Using the low-level API is not recommended. Use Register<T>() or Register<T>(ref T) instead. SaveAsJson If true save config as JSON. If false saves as binary. bool SaveAsJson { get; set; } Property Value bool Examples Config.SaveAsJson = true; Remarks Warning JSON is not fully readable and only ment for debugging. Methods GetSettings() Returns all settings, for config UI. IReadOnlyDictionary<string, object> GetSettings() Returns IReadOnlyDictionary<string, object> Examples var settings = Config.GetSettings(); OnChanged(string, Action<object>) Subscribe to see if a value changed. void OnChanged(string key, Action<object> callback) Parameters key string callback Action<object> Examples Config.OnChanged("Key", (val) => { }); Remarks Warning Using the low-level API is not recommended. Use Register<T>() or Register<T>(ref T) instead. Read<T>(string, T) Read value from config. T Read<T>(string key, T defaultValue = default) Parameters key string defaultValue T Returns T Type Parameters T Examples var val = Config.Read<int>("Key"); var val = Config.Read("Key", 1.0f); Remarks Warning Using the low-level API is not recommended. Use Register<T>() or Register<T>(ref T) instead. Register<T>() Register a typed config class and get back a handle. Lunaris will build the settings UI automatically. IConfigHandle<T> Register<T>() where T : class, new() Returns IConfigHandle<T> Type Parameters T Examples var config = Config.Register<MySettings>(); Register<T>(ref T) Register a typed config class and get back a handle. Also assigns the loaded config to instance. Lunaris will build the settings UI automatically. IConfigHandle<T> Register<T>(ref T instance) where T : class, new() Parameters instance T Returns IConfigHandle<T> Type Parameters T Examples var config = Config.Register(ref instance); var config = Config.Register<MySettings>(ref instance); Reset() Resets config to default values. void Reset() Examples Config.Reset(); Save() Save config. Notice: Writing automatically saves. void Save() Examples Config.Save(); SetDesc(string, string) Set description for key. void SetDesc(string key, string desc) Parameters key string desc string Examples Config.SetDesc("Key", "This value changes how..."); Remarks Warning Using the low-level API is not recommended. Use Register<T>() or Register<T>(ref T) instead. SetRange(string, float, float) Set range for key. void SetRange(string key, float min, float max) Parameters key string min float max float Examples Config.SetRange("Key", 0f, 10f); Remarks Warning Using the low-level API is not recommended. Use Register<T>() or Register<T>(ref T) instead. SetSection(string, string) Set section for key. void SetSection(string key, string section) Parameters key string section string Examples Config.SetSection("Key", "SectionName"); Remarks Warning Using the low-level API is not recommended. Use Register<T>() or Register<T>(ref T) instead. Write<T>(string, T) Write value to config. void Write<T>(string key, T value) Parameters key string value T Type Parameters T Examples Config.Write("Key", 2.0f); Remarks Warning Using the low-level API is not recommended. Use Register<T>() or Register<T>(ref T) instead.

api/Lunaris.IConfig.html.title: Interface IConfig | Lunaris
api/Lunaris.IConfig.html.summary: Interface IConfig Namespace Lunaris Assembly Lunaris.dll public interface IConfig Properties OnAnyChanged Subscribe to ALL changes. Action<string, object> OnAnyChanged { get; set; } Property Value Action<string, object> Examples Config.OnAnyChanged += (key, val) => { }; SaveAsJson If true save config as JSON. If false saves as binary. bool SaveAsJson { get; set; } Property Value bool Examples Config.SaveAsJson = true; Methods GetSettings() Returns all settings, for config UI. IReadOnlyDictionary<string, object> GetSettings() Returns IReadOnlyDictionary<string, object> Examples var settings = Config.GetSettings(); OnChanged(string, Action<object>) Subscribe to see if a value changed. void OnChanged(string key, Action<object> callback) Parameters key string callback Action<object> Examples Config.OnChanged("Key", (val) => { }); Read<T>(string, T) Read value from config. T Read<T>(string key, T defaultValue = default) Parameters key string defaultValue T Returns T Type Parameters T Examples var val = Config.Read<int>("Key"); var val = Config.Read("Key", 1.0f); Register<T>() Register a typed config class and get back a handle. The loader will introspect T to build a settings UI automatically. IConfigHandle<T> Register<T>() where T : class, new() Returns IConfigHandle<T> Type Parameters T Examples var config = Config.Register<MySettings>(); Reset() Resets config to default values. void Reset() Examples Config.Reset(); Save() Save config. Notice: Writing automatically saves. void Save() Examples Config.Save(); Write<T>(string, T) Write value to config. void Write<T>(string key, T value) Parameters key string value T Type Parameters T Examples Config.Write("Key", 2.0f);

api/Lunaris.IConfigHandle-1.html.summary: Interface IConfigHandle<T> Namespace Lunaris Assembly Lunaris.dll Handle returned by Register<T>(). public interface IConfigHandle<T> where T : class, new() Type Parameters T Methods Get() Get current config. Returns full config. event Action<T> OnAnyChanged Event Type Action<T> Examples config.OnAnyChanged += (cfg) => {};

api/Lunaris.LunarisCommandAttribute.html.title: Class LunarisCommandAttribute | Lunaris
api/Lunaris.LunarisCommandAttribute.html.summary: Class LunarisCommandAttribute Namespace Lunaris Assembly Lunaris.dll Marks a method as a Command, automatically registering it with the command system and making it visible in the plugin installer. All registered plugin commands can also be seen by typing /lunaris help. [AttributeUsage(AttributeTargets.Method)] public sealed class LunarisCommandAttribute : Attribute, _Attribute Inheritance object Attribute LunarisCommandAttribute Implements _Attribute Examples [LunarisCommand("heal", "Heals the player by the specified amount")] public void HealCommand(float amount) { player.Heal(amount); } // In chat: /myplugin heal 50 Remarks The command is registered in chat as /pluginname commandname, where pluginname comes from the LunarisPluginAttribute name (spaces removed and converted to lowercase). The method's parameters determine the command's arguments, and only the following types are supported: string int float Warning The method must match a supported parameter signature (0-8 args) and params. Using unsupported types will throw an ArgumentException. Using more than 8 parameters will throw a NotSupportedException. Properties Description public string Description { get; } Property Value string Exceptions ArgumentException Thrown if a parameter type is not one of the supported types (string, int, float). NotSupportedException Thrown if the method has more than 8 parameters. Name public string Name { get; } Property Value string Exceptions ArgumentException Thrown if a parameter type is not one of the supported types (string, int, float). NotSupportedException Thrown if the method has more than 8 parameters.

index.html.title: | Lunaris
index.html.summary: You can still use BepInEx modding with Lunaris, however switching to the Lunaris API is recommended for full compatibility and speed. Currently Lunaris does not support load ordering so keep this in mind, it is however planned for a future release. Lunaris also comes with a built-in console(WIP) and unity explorer, use the dev bar to access these. Please read this Documentation to learn about specific implementation details. If you want Lunaris to associate your manually installed mod with the Vault, add the following attribute with your mods Vault ID: [assembly: System.Reflection.AssemblyMetadata("LunarisPluginId", "vaultID")] Included Libraries Lunaris ships with the following, you don't need to bundle them yourself: Mono.Cecil MonoMod Newtonsoft.Json ImGui.NET dear ImGui 0Harmony BepInEx (modified, for wrapping only) You can download all libraries except BepInEx here. Getting Started Reference Lunaris.dll in your project. Your plugin class needs to extend LunarisPlugin and requires the [LunarisPlugin] attribute, similar to BepInEx. [LunarisPlugin("ModName", "SemVerVersion", "AuthorName", "ShortDescription")] [LunarisPermission(LunarisPermission.None)] public class MyMod : LunarisPlugin { void Awake() { Logging.Log("Hello from MyMod!"); } } For testing, you can simply replace your dll during runtime inside the plugins folder, unless you have disabled it, Lunaris will automatically reload your mod. Permissions The permission attribute is not required, and currently doesn't serve a real purpose besides letting the user know that you did not add it. Commands [LunarisCommand("test", "A test command.")] public static void Command_Test(string name) { // /myplugin test somestring } When adding a command, Lunaris takes your sanitized plugin name (set through the [LunarisPlugin] attribute) and automatically registers commands in-game. You no longer need to manually hook CheckCommands. Config The preferred way to use configs with Lunaris is through Config.Register<T>(). Lunaris also implements a more low-level way, please see the docs for more details. IPC (Aura) Lunaris includes an IPC system for communication between mods without directly referencing each other. However, this does require mods to actually expose endpoints. Cleanup Since Lunaris allows unloading mods during runtime you need to ensure to clean up everything during OnDestroy(). Unity does not allow assemblies to live outside of its own context, which makes it incredibly hard to remove your work from memory. Not cleaning up after yourself means you are directly causing memory leaks, ESPECIALLY when using ImGui.

packages/MonoMod.Backports.1.1.2/README..title: MonoMod.Backports | Lunaris
packages/MonoMod.Backports.1.1.2/README..summary: MonoMod.Backports MonoMod.Backports is a collection of backports of new library features from new versions of the .NET BCL to all of the versions which MonoMod targets. This includes several extra types which expose functionality which was added to existing types. As long as MonoMod.Backports targets the most recent framework version, all code which uses it will use the library features actually provided by the runtime. Notable APIs MonoMod.Backports.MethodImplOptionsEx - This can be used everywhere in a project with global using MethodImplOptions = MonoMod.Backports.MethodImplOptionsEx;

packages/MonoMod.Core.1.1.2/README.html.title: | Lunaris
packages/MonoMod.Core.1.1.2/README.html.summary: THIS IS PROBABLY NOT THE LIBRARY YOU WANT TO BE USING. You probably want RuntimeDetour instead. Notable APIs MonoMod.Core.DetourFactory.Current MonoMod.Core.ICoreDetour Usage Use DetourFactory.Current.CreateDetour to create a single detour from one method to another. The detour will be automatically undone when the returnedc object is disposed or garbage collected. Only one such detour may be made per method. If multiple are made, they will not be cleaned up properly. MonoMod.Core does not track which methods have already been detoured, and will not throw. It is therefore highly recommended to use a higher-level detouring API (like that provided by MonoMod.RuntimeDetour or Harmony) to perform detours. Those higher level APIs also provide solutions to many of the limitations to Core's detour abstraction, such as the ability to call the original, unmodified method, or modify the IL of the method. (See their documentation for how this is actually done.) Additionally, interfaces in this package may have members added across minor version updates. Other version-based compatability guarantees are retained. Other potentially useful APIs The default IDetourFactory utilizes MonoMod.Core.Platforms.PlatformTriple.Current to implement detours. Notably, that factory handles re-creating the underlying ISimpleNativeDetour objects when methods are recompiled by the runtime. PlatformTriple does provide a few other utilities which may be useful for a higher-level library, however: PlatformTriple.Prepare JIT compiles a method, sully supporting generic instantiations PlatformTriple.GetIdentifiable translates the provided MethodBase into an instance which can be used as, for instance, the key to a ConditionalWeakTable The other methods of PlatformTriple are means of implementing the detour factory, and likely should not be touched.
